summary: Deep audit of click-to-open surfaces (dialog/popover/sheet/dropdown) for leftover same-shape-different-code spacing debt. Evidence only, no migration.
logs: audited 22/22 files — 16 MISS findings across 10 files

findings.shape: drag-drop file upload target (icon circle + title + hint, dashed border)
findings.classString: flex w-full flex-col items-center justify-center gap-1.5 rounded-md border border-dashed px-4 py-6 text-center transition-colors
findings.why: Genuine one-off compound interaction (native drag events + click-to-pick + hidden file input). No owner shape in the vocabulary targets a drop zone; census explicitly lists drag-drop upload targets under 'genuinely one-off compound blocks'.

findings.shape: selected-file summary card (icon box + filename/size + remove button)
findings.classString: flex items-center gap-3 rounded-md border border-border/60 bg-background p-2.5
findings.why: Superficially a leading/content/trailing 3-slot row like SettingsRow, but SettingsRow never owns its own border/rounded/background (mx-4 + border-b hairline, relies on the parent SettingsSection card) and uses min-h-[3.75rem] + asymmetric py-3/mx-4. This is a single floating bordered card with uniform p-2.5 padding and no card parent -- an isolated one-item summary, not a divider row in a list of siblings. Different relationship (standalone card vs list-membership row), same rough leading/body/trailing skeleton for a different reason (exactly one item, never repeats).

findings.shape: 'reading backup' loading row (spinner + text)
findings.classString: flex items-center gap-2 rounded-md border border-border/60 bg-background p-3 text-xs text-muted-foreground
findings.why: Centered loading placeholder inside a bordered box so the dialog body does not reflow while the preview call is in flight. Matches the census's explicit stays-local bucket for loading/skeleton placeholders.

findings.shape: preview-error inline message
findings.classString: rounded-md border border-destructive/30 bg-destructive/5 p-3 text-xs text-destructive
findings.why: Same destructive alpha convention CalloutBanner uses (border-destructive/30 bg-destructive/5), but it is a single unstructured message string with no icon, no title/description split, and rounded-md (not CalloutBanner's rounded-[var(--radius-menu-shell)]). CalloutBanner's contract is icon+title(bold)+description+trailing-action; this is one plain sentence. Reusing CalloutBanner here would force an empty title slot and an unused icon for a one-line status string -- a different relationship (raw alert text vs a titled notice card).

findings.shape: backend conflict message loop (v-for preview.conflicts)
findings.classString: rounded-md border border-destructive/30 bg-destructive/5 p-2.5 text-[11px] text-destructive
findings.why: Same reasoning as the preview-error box above: a repeated one-line message, no title/description/icon structure, smaller padding/text scale than CalloutBanner. Not the CalloutBanner morphology.

findings.shape: encrypted-bundle passphrase unlock block (icon+title, hint paragraph, inline Input+Button row)
findings.classString: space-y-2 rounded-md border border-border/60 bg-background p-3
findings.why: Has no <Label> element at all and is not a Label-over-control field -- FieldStack's contract is Label (bound via for) then control then optional help text, in that vertical order. Here it's an icon-title line, then a muted hint paragraph, then a horizontal Input+Button action row (type passphrase, press unlock) -- an inline unlock affordance, not a form field. Also the whole thing is its own bordered card, matching the census's own listed one-off: 'bot-import-panel + bot-backup-actions dialog-body compounds with their own denser language'.

findings.shape: create vs overwrite mode selector
findings.classString: grid w-full grid-cols-2 (TabsList inside Tabs v-model="importMode")
findings.why: Plain @memohai/ui Tabs/TabsList/TabsTrigger atom composed as-is with no hand-rolled spacing wrapper. Not an owner-shape candidate.

findings.shape: profile identity selection card (avatar + name/hint, conditional click-to-toggle, trailing check-circle)
findings.classString: flex items-center gap-3 rounded-md border px-3 py-2.5 transition-colors
findings.why: Looks like BackendCard's leading/content/trailing horizontal object row, but BackendCard's contract is always a <button> whose click navigates/opens something, root radius rounded-[var(--radius-menu-shell)], padding p-3.5, trailing defaults to a ChevronRight. Here the root is a <div> that is only conditionally clickable (only when isOverwrite), padding is px-3 py-2.5, radius is plain rounded-md, and the trailing element is a custom check-circle indicating a toggled selection state, not navigation. This is the same 'selection card' family as the sibling BackupSectionCards item cards (already ruled a genuinely different dense selection-card morphology by the census for backup-section-cards.vue) rather than a BackendCard occurrence.

findings.shape: upload/import percent progress bar
findings.classString: space-y-1 (row) + h-1 overflow-hidden rounded-full bg-muted (track) + h-full rounded-full bg-primary transition-all duration-200 (fill)
findings.why: One-off percent progress indicator tied to XHR upload progress; no owner shape in the vocabulary targets a progress bar.

findings.shape: bottom action bar (Cancel/Import buttons, border-top)
findings.classString: shrink-0 space-y-2 border-t pt-3 ... flex justify-end gap-2
findings.why: Visually resembles SettingsSection's #footer slot (border-t, right-aligned button row), but SettingsSection's footer lives INSIDE a bordered card (border-t border-border px-4 py-3, full-bleed to the card's own edges) and this panel is not wrapped in a SettingsSection at all -- it's a raw flex-column dialog body with no card shell to attach a footer slot to. Forcing SettingsSection here would mean restructuring the whole panel into a section card, a much larger change than filling a slot. This reads as generic dialog-footer geometry, not a hand-rolled SettingsSection footer.

results.summary: This file is clean of genuine same-shape-different-code misses. Every row/card/banner-like shape in bot-import-panel.vue was re-examined against its nearest owner candidate (SettingsRow, CalloutBanner, FieldStack, BackendCard, SettingsSection footer) and each one differs in a load-bearing way: no shared card parent (so no mx-4/border-b hairline relationship), own border+radius+background per box, uniform rather than asymmetric padding, plain-text alerts with no title/description/icon split, a toggle-card rather than a navigation card, and a compound icon+hint+inline-input unlock block with no Label element at all. This matches the owner-vocabulary census's own explicit note that bot-import-panel (together with bot-backup-actions) is a 'dialog-body compound with its own denser language' that was deliberately left local rather than migrated in Phase 3 -- this line-by-line read confirms that call rather than overturning it. Three findings (the selected-file summary card, the profile identity toggle card, and the bottom action bar) are flagged needsHumanEye=true because their resemblance to SettingsRow/BackendCard/SettingsSection-footer is close enough that a rendered side-by-side comparison would be a more reliable tie-breaker than reading classes alone, but by geometry and by contract (card ownership, click semantics, padding scale) all three land as different relationships, not misses. No new owner shapes are proposed.

results.summary: This file (plus its two always-inlined dialog-body children, backup-section-cards.vue and bot-import-panel.vue, which only render after the Export/Import buttons are clicked) is clean of owner-vocabulary MISSes. Every shape that superficially pattern-matches an owner (BackupSectionCards' per-section rows, the passphrase field, the selected-file chip, the profile identity card) uses a deliberately denser dialog-body scale — text-[11px]/text-[10px]/text-xs labels, h-8 inputs, px-3 py-2.5 compact cards — that is consistently different from the settings-page contract (SettingsRow's min-h-[3.75rem]/py-3/mx-4-inset geometry, FieldStack's text-body Label + h-9 control + text-xs help). This matches the owner-vocabulary census's own explicit finding, written before this audit: 'bot-import-panel + bot-backup-actions dialog-body compounds with their own denser language' were confirmed stays-local at migration time, not overlooked. I independently re-derived the same conclusion by reading every interactive surface line-by-line rather than trusting the census's prior note at face value, and found nothing it missed. One item (the selected-file summary row) is flagged needsHumanEye=true only because its resemblance to a SettingsRow #leading/#content/trailing arrangement is closer than the others, though I still judge it DIFF-RELATIONSHIP on geometry grounds. Zero MISS findings — this file does not need migration work.

results.summary: This file is clean of spacing-owner debt — zero MISS findings. It has no click-to-open surfaces of its own (no Dialog/Sheet/Popover/DropdownMenu); it IS itself the dense compound content that renders inside two parent dialogs (bot-backup-actions.vue, bot-import-panel.vue), exactly as the owner-vocabulary census already documented. Every shape in the file (the outer 3-state selection card, its internal row, the segmented strategy selector, the expand chevron, the circular check indicator, and the expanded mono detail list) is a deliberately denser, self-contained visual language purpose-built for a compact multi-item backup/restore selector — none of it matches the geometry of SettingsRow (fails min-h-[3.75rem]/py-3/mx-4 inset-divider), ExpandableSettingsRow (no SettingsRow-shaped header under the chevron), FieldStack (no label-over-control fields, only read-only metadata), MetricReadout, BackendCard, PersonaTile, or CalloutBanner. This confirms rather than contradicts the census's prior conclusion for this exact file. Two non-spacing observations are flagged needsHumanEye for a separate control-reuse pass (not spacing debt): the hand-rolled 3-way segmented strategy pill (line 183) duplicates @memohai/ui SegmentedControl, and the circular check indicator (line 217) duplicates @memohai/ui Checkbox — both are out of scope for the spacing-owner vocabulary but worth a maintainer decision on a future non-spacing cleanup pass.
